definitions.text: The reflexive case of they, the third-person plural personal pronoun. The group of people, animals, or objects previously mentioned, as the object of a verb or following a preposition .
The reflexive case of they, the third-person singular personal pronoun. The single person previously mentioned, as the object of a verb or following a preposition .
examples: Would whoever stole my phone please make themselves known.
I don't want anyone to fight among themselves.
